channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Former HHS Secretary Xavier Becerra will face Hilton in California's race for governor
item.description: Nearly a week after election day, Xavier Becerra, former Secretary of Health and Human Services, will face Republican Steve Hilton in the California's race for governor.
item.content:encoded: Nearly a week after election day, Xavier Becerra, former Secretary of Health and Human Services, will face Republican Steve Hilton in the California's race for governor.
item.dc:creator: Guy Marzorati

item.title: House passes bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the remainder of Trump's term
item.description: The bill provides roughly $70 billion for immigration enforcement and highlights a GOP caucus continuing to endorse Trump's immigration agenda as Democrats warn Congress has ceded its oversight role.
item.content:encoded:
The bill provides roughly $70 billion for immigration enforcement and highlights a GOP caucus continuing to endorse Trump's immigration agenda as Democrats warn Congress has ceded its oversight role.

(Image credit: Mariam Zuhaib)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: China re-centers North Korea ties as nuclear silence reshapes balance
item.description: North Korea's Kim Jong Un rebalances ties between China and Russia, and gets fresh pledges of support from Beijing, while keeping his nuclear weapons off the agenda.
item.content:encoded: North Korea's Kim Jong Un rebalances ties between China and Russia, and gets fresh pledges of support from Beijing, while keeping his nuclear weapons off the agenda.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: President Trump says U.S. 'must' respond to Iran attack that downed Army helicopter
item.description: Monday night, President Trump spoke optimistically about negotiations to end the war with Iran. Now, he has confirmed that Iran shot down a U.S. Army Apache helicopter, saying the U.S. must respond.
item.content:encoded: Monday night, President Trump spoke optimistically about negotiations to end the war with Iran. Now, he has confirmed that Iran shot down a U.S. Army Apache helicopter, saying the U.S. must respond.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Trump has been trying to secure a better nuclear deal with Iran than Obama. Can he?
item.description: For years, President Trump slammed former President Obama's nuclear deal with Iran. Now he has to push through his own.
item.content:encoded: For years, President Trump slammed former President Obama's nuclear deal with Iran. Now he has to push through his own.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Nollywood's new screen: Nigerian filmmakers skip cinemas for YouTube
item.description: In Nigeria, a film revolution is unfolding — not in cinemas, but on YouTube.
item.content:encoded: In Nigeria, a film revolution is unfolding — not in cinemas, but on YouTube.
item.dc:creator: Jewel Bright

item.title: San Francisco immigration court shuts down, striking at heart of historic advocacy
item.description: San Francisco was the gateway to the U.S. for millions of immigrants. The closure of its immigration court affects hundreds of thousands of immigration cases and deals a symbolic blow to advocates.
item.content:encoded: San Francisco was the gateway to the U.S. for millions of immigrants. The closure of its immigration court affects hundreds of thousands of immigration cases and deals a symbolic blow to advocates.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: 'Contrapposto' portrays a tangled relationship, set amidst the art world
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang chats with author Dave Eggers about his new novel Contrapposto.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang chats with author Dave Eggers about his new novel Contrapposto.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan

item.title: From side gigs to canceling trips, Americans adjust to months of high gas prices
item.description: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.content:encoded: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Fuming in N.J.: As World Cup costs add up, some question whether hosting is worth it
item.description: With only days to go before the World Cup, some people in New Jersey are fuming about how much the tournament is going to cost the state — and making their feelings known.
item.content:encoded: With only days to go before the World Cup, some people in New Jersey are fuming about how much the tournament is going to cost the state — and making their feelings known.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam
